fills: Tabel 4.1 / 4.1.1.3 external dataset registry + per-dataset counts + synthetic/replay corpus counts
data_class: LIVE registry + LIVE per-dataset counts (external_log_attempts, current DB). The registry itself is static metadata; counts are current-DB snapshots.
registry_source_sql: SELECT dataset_id, family, concept_prefix, citation, license, metadata->>'source_sha256_combined' FROM external_dataset_registry ORDER BY dataset_id
counts_source_sql: SELECT dataset_id, count(DISTINCT source_user_id) src_learners, count(DISTINCT user_id) mapped_learners, count(*) attempts FROM external_log_attempts GROUP BY dataset_id
g1_note: source_sha256_combined (G1) is a 12-char prefix as stored in external_dataset_registry.metadata; it is the content fingerprint of the combined source files for that dataset.

datasets.license: Public research dataset (per ASSISTments terms of use).

datasets.citation: Edwards, S.H., Murali, K.P., Tilden, M. et al. (2020). The Computer Science Education Data Mining (CSEDM) Data Challenge.
datasets.license: Public research dataset (per CSEDM Data Challenge terms).

datasets.citation: Choi, Y., Lee, Y., Cho, J., Baek, J., Kim, B., Cha, Y., Shin, D., Bae, C., Heo, J. (2020). EdNet: A Large-Scale Hierarchical Dataset in Education. arXiv:1912.03072.
Chang, H.-S., Hsu, H.-J., Chen, K.-T. (2015). Modeling Exercise Relationships in E-Learning: A Unified Approach. Proceedings of the 8th International Conference on Educational Data Mining (EDM 2015).
datasets.license: Research use only — no commercial usage permitted.

datasets.citation: Chang, H.-S., Hsu, H.-J., Chen, K.-T. (2015). Modeling Exercise Relationships in E-Learning: A Unified Approach. Proceedings of the 8th International Conference on Educational Data Mining (EDM 2015).
datasets.license: Research use only — no commercial usage permitted.
datasets.note: junyi_2015_graph is the dominant corpus and the family the sealed anchor (run-d2154070) draws its learners from (user_id prefix run-94a3b8ba::ex_junyi_graph_*).

totals.counts_caveat: src_learners = distinct source_user_id (original dataset learner ids); mapped_learners = distinct user_id after the platform's id-mapping (a single source learner can map to multiple platform user_ids across runs, which is why mapped >= src for most datasets). attempts = total ingested rows in external_log_attempts for that dataset.

synthetic_run_count.sql: SELECT count(DISTINCT experiment_run_id) FROM experiment_trajectories WHERE synthetic = true AND experiment_run_id LIKE 'run-%'
synthetic_run_count.synthetic_rows_sql: SELECT count(*) FROM experiment_trajectories WHERE synthetic = true
synthetic_run_count.note: 33 = distinct clean run-<uuid> experiment runs flagged synthetic=true. The synthetic flag marks brain-generated (non-replay, non-human) cohort traffic.

replay_corpus_count.note: Replay corpus is the sealed-run ledger (3 seals) plus their fork lineage (6 fork edges in run_forks). The two replay traffic classes in experiment_trajectories (replay_kalman_canonical=122316 rows, replay_v2_causal=122316 rows) are the materialized replays driven from these sealed parents.
